item.text: I’ll inspect the finding and the relevant code paths first, then patch the smallest surface that removes the vulnerability while preserving behavior.

item.text: I found two weak checks: discovery accepts non-HTTPS metadata/JWKS endpoints, and MCP validation only compares issuer when both configured issuer and token issuer are non-empty. I’m checking tests now so I can tighten those checks without breaking the mock-server coverage unintentionally.

I’m going to add explicit endpoint validation for discovered OIDC metadata and JWKS URLs, allowing cleartext only for loopback test/dev servers, and then make issuer validation fail closed whenever discovery provided an issuer. This preserves the existing local test setup while removing the production auth bypass.

The code now rejects non-HTTPS auth endpoints except loopback and requires an issuer from discovery. I’m running the focused test package to catch any behavior assumptions in the existing generic auth tests.

item.text: I’m adding regression coverage at the helper level for endpoint scheme validation and at claim validation for the missing-issuer bypass. That keeps the tests focused on the vulnerability rather than requiring a full TLS JWKS setup.

The focused regression tests are in place. I’m scanning the edited sections and the package diff now for formatting issues and unintended changes since automated formatting is unavailable in this environment.

The diff is limited to the generic auth package. One concern remains: the existing tests use HTTP loopback JWKS servers, so the loopback allowance is deliberate; production/non-loopback HTTP is now rejected.

item.text: I’m tightening two adjacent auth paths as well: configured/discovered introspection endpoints get the same URL validation, and non-MCP JWT validation now enforces the discovered issuer just like MCP JWT validation. Both are part of the same trust boundary.
I’ve extended the remediation to cover the non-MCP JWT path. I’m adding a regression case so a token signed by the trusted JWKS but carrying the wrong issuer is rejected.
The patch is complete. I’m doing a final diff review and another attempt to locate a runnable Go toolchain; if it’s still unavailable, I’ll leave the exact verification gap in the handoff.

item.text:
Implemented the remediation in [generic.go](/work/internal/auth/generic/generic.go):

- Rejects non-HTTPS auth, JWKS, and introspection endpoints, except loopback HTTP for local/test servers.
- Requires OIDC discovery metadata to include an issuer.
- Enforces issuer validation for both MCP claim validation and non-MCP JWT header validation, including rejecting missing issuer claims when an issuer was discovered.

Added regression coverage in [generic_test.go](/work/internal/auth/generic/generic_test.go) for endpoint URL validation, issuer mismatch, and missing issuer rejection.

Verification:
- Ran `/usr/local/go/bin/gofmt` successfully.
- Ran `git diff --check` successfully.
- Tried `/usr/local/go/bin/go test ./internal/auth/generic`, but it could not compile because dependency downloads from `proxy.golang.org` are blocked by the environment egress allowlist.
